title: pavetta orthanthera
intro: Pavetta orthanthera, also known as the Indian tea tree, is a plant species native to Sri Lanka and parts of India, characterized by its clusters of white or pink flowers and its woody stem. The medicinal properties of Pavetta orthanthera lie in its leaves, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The health benefits of this plant include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help to reduce pain and inflammation, as well as protect against cell damage. Herbal preparations of Pavetta orthanthera include infusions made from its leaves, which can be consumed as a tea or used as a topical treatment for skin conditions.
